key_insight: Riazi-8B shows that Urdu mathematical reasoning can be improved by combining Urdu language adaptation with reasoning-focused fine-tuning.
engineering_relevance: The paper offers a practical adaptation recipe for extending step-by-step math problem solving to low-resource languages beyond English.
method: Continued pre-training on Urdu Wikipedia to strengthen Urdu language understanding
Supervised fine-tuning on Urdu Chain-of-Thought data derived from GSM8K
Evaluation on MGSM-Urdu against existing Urdu instruction-tuned models
evidence_signal: The model reports consistent gains in answer correctness, reasoning quality, response completeness, and Urdu generation on MGSM-Urdu.
watch_item: The approach depends on translated or adapted CoT data and may still be limited by the scarcity and quality of Urdu reasoning benchmarks.
paper_title: Riazi-8B: An Urdu Large Language Model for Mathematical Reasoning
